notes.anchor: to throw men from the rock
notes.note: The Tarpeian Rock, the cliff of the Capitol from which traitors and murderers were hurled; Cicero applauds the consul Dolabella for executing in this brutal traditional way the mob that had been venerating Caesar's makeshift altar.
A column and altar to Caesar set up in the Forum by the freedman Herophilus (Amatius) and the people; Dolabella demolished it and dispersed the cult, an act Cicero calls "heroic."
The Liberators Brutus, Cassius, and their fellow assassins of Caesar; Cicero feared the popular grief for Caesar, if left to fester, would endanger them.
Pilia, Atticus's wife; Cicero, leaving for Pompeii, is handing the villa and its contents back into her keeping.
The first of May (1 May 44 BC), the day Cicero set out for his villa at Pompeii; a calendar date.
